observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to Management:

Send confirmation letter to the Bureau of Treasury to determine the existence and validity of the Due from NGAs account;

Send demand letters to the beneficiaries of the loan granted; and

Determine the status of collectability of these accounts and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended the following to Management:

The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the status of projects recorded under the CIP accounts and reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate PPE accounts. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant reclassify projects recorded under the CIP to its appropriate PPE account upon completion of the project; 

Consider any possible action on how to find or raise funds in order to continue the construction of all idle projects, like that of the Fitness Gym as above noted; and

As much as possible, consider the usability of all projects especially those whose construction are made by phases, in order to maximize the benefits of the same and avoid wastage of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position and the Notes to FS for fair presentation of the accounts in the FS.

We recommended the following to Management:

Provide the Audit Team with an update on the aforementioned DA IX funded projects including availability of funds and submit necessary documents like the Memorandum of Agreement and project guidelines from the source agency;

Send communication both to the source agency and the contractor to clarify matters pertaining to the said projects and to gather data and/or information on the possible plan of action to resolve the issue; and

The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the actual status of the projects for the possible transfer of the same from CIP accounts to its appropriate asset account; and recording of Accounts Payable, if warranted.

We recommended the following to management:

The RHU to make proper and immediate coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accounting Office for recording of received medical items; and

The Municipal Accounting Office and the RHU coordinate with IPHO Zamboanga del Sur and DOH and seek clarification for the proper recording of the received Covid Vaccines, Drugs and Medicine, and other medical supplies.
We recommended the management to submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents within the reglementary period.

We recommended the following to management:

Submit a copy of the Transmittal Letters on the liquidation of cash advances for confidential funds including copies of transmittal letters in compliance with the audit recommendation of COA-ICFAU, if any, and make appropriate and complete disclosure of said information in the Notes to FS; and

Consider the submission of a request for write-off following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the following applicable conditions:
1. Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts
2. Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor
3. Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her
4. Incapacity to pay or insolvency
5. Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers
6. No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts

In addition, we recommended that management take necessary action in case accountable officers failed to respond to the demand letter, particularly those who were already separated from the LGU.

We recommended that management submit a supplemental budget, if there is any, to support the said expenditure, and if there is none, identify which item in the 2020 Annual Budget these penalties were charged and submit a written explanation for the incurrence and payment of penalties.

We recommended the management to promulgate rules, regulations and guidelines on rental of heavy equipment and other LGU properties for proper recording and reporting, and to safeguard said properties from possible loss or damage; and

We recommended the following to management:

Submit the lacking documents pertaining to the above-cited projects and of all other projects implemented in CY 2019 according to the sequence cited in the schedule above with proper tabbing for easy referencing; and

Strictly observed the submission of required documents within the prescribed reglementary period for all future procurements for timely, systematic and effective review and evaluation of the project.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to the following:
The Office of the GSO to immediately conduct full physical count on the inventories in the SEF books and to submit an updated RPCI; and,
The Office of the Municipal Accountant and Office of the GSO to conduct periodic reconciliation on the accounting records with the results of the actual physical count based on the RPCI, to include updating the cost per stock item therein.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Accountant to :
Make prior period adjustments to the noted overstatements of revenues in the General Fund and SEF; and,
To record transactions of RPT and SET using NGAS for LGUs, instead of the GAM for LGUs.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to deposit his collection regularly and intact as prescribed under relevant laws and rules and adopt necessary control measures in the handling of deposits of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to the risk of possible loss and misuse.

Recommendation
We reiterated, and the Management agreed to our previous recommendations as follows:
Send confirmation letter to the Bureau of Treasury/GSIS to determine the existence and validity of the Due from NGAs account for the GSIS Contribution withheld from the Internal Revenue Allotment;
Send demand letters to the beneficiaries of the loan granted; and
Determine the status of collectability of these accounts and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the Municipal Accountant to present the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account in the Statement of Financial Position for fair presentation of the accounts in the FS.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Initiate the request for write-off of dormant Due to NGAs account balances in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and,
Make representations with identified source agencies for the return of unutilized balances and/or reversion of the same to the National Treasury, as warranted.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed on the following:
The Accounting Unit to immediately conduct reconciliation on its existing records and exert efforts to locate errors made in the recording of transactions;
The Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entry/ies to correct the deficiency; and,
Ensure correct recording of transactions in the appropriate FS accounts and its corresponding normal balances in the FS.

Recommendation
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare and include the required reconciliation in the Notes to FS for any material differences of actual amounts between SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance based on the information obtained from the Municipal Budget Officer.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed that through the Municipal Development Council:
Provide specific timelines for the period of implementation of the PPAs;
Ensure that PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and -implementation-ready; and,
Conduct review and evaluation of PPAs and consider changes/realignments in the budget for projects which cannot be implemented or have residual balances during the year to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan.

Recommendation
We recommended the GAD Focal Person the following:
Maximize the utilization of GAD Funds through employing effective and efficient measures in the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects to attain the objectives in which funds were provided; and
For those PPAs already implemented but still have unutilized amounts, consider utilizing those amounts to implement new GAD-related PPAs to ensure more benefits to be derived for the intended constituents.

Recommendation
We recommended that the LSB submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the timelines.

Recommendation
We recommended that the LSB ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports at the website of the LGU and/or in at least three conspicuous public places and that the same be made not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021. Furthermore, ensure that postings are readable by the public in order to promote accountability and transparency.

We recommended the following to Management:

If liquidation is still not achieved despite receipt of demand letter by the accountable officer, consider withholding the salaries or any amount due to the latter until balances have been fully settled;

Immediately furnish the audit team with copies of the Demand Letter and Withholding Order duly received by the concerned accountable officers; and

Initiate action to recover unsettled CAs of personnel who were already separated from service upon failure by the latter to respond to demand letters.

We recommended the following to Management:

The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to endorse the opening of Bank Account for the unexpended QRF and LDRRMF – MOOE to the LGU’s Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the issuance of an SB resolution;

The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and LDRRM Officer to facilitate the immediate transfer of any unexpended balance of LDRRMF-QRF/MOOE to the Special Trust Fund books and bank account; and

The Municipal Treasurer effect the cash transfer for the unexpended balance and the Municipal Accountant record the transfer of Cash from the General Fund books to the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits his collection regularly and intact as prescribed and to adopt necessary control measures in the handling of deposits of cash collections so as not to expose the scarce government monies to the risk of possible loss and misuse.

We recommended the MEEDO submit quarterly report on uncollected water fees and stall rentals to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and to the Municipal Accounting Office for proper monitoring of income and receivables, and recording of the same in the books of the LGU.

We recommended the following to management:

Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;

Inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and 

Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

We recommended management to consider the imposition of liquidated damages to the supplier on the delayed delivery of the ordered items and submit a computation to the audit team for the amount of possible liquidated damages.

We recommended the following to management:

The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for setting up the RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred Credits at the beginning of the year;

The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the names of delinquent taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible; and


The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The RHU to make proper and immediate coordination with the GSO and Municipal Accounting Office for recording of received medical items; and

The Municipal Accounting Office and the RHU coordinate with IPHO Zamboanga del Sur and DOH and seek clarification for the proper recording of the received Covid Vaccines, Drugs and Medicine, and other medical supplies.

We recommended the following to management:

The MEEDO submit quarterly report on uncollected water fees and stall rentals to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and to the Municipal Accounting Office for proper monitoring of income and receivables, and recording of the same in the books of the LGU; and

The Municipal Accountant draw journal entries to reflect the necessary adjustments to the income and receivable accounts based on the reports received from the MEEDO.

We recommended the following to management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. No.1445 shall be imposed; and

Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents within the reglementary period.

Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;

Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and 

Prepare and maintain a monthly project monitoring report of the PPAs.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations to management:
Continue the practice of sending demand letters to those with outstanding cash advances and require them to settle unliquidated CAs. Make follow-up demand if necessary. If liquidation is still not achieved, consider withholding their salaries or any amount due them until balances have been fully settled;
Submit a copy of the Transmittal Letters on the liquidation of cash advances for confidential funds including copies of transmittal letters in compliance with the audit recommendation of COA-ICFAU, if any, and make appropriate and complete disclosure of said information in the Notes to FS; and
Consider the submission of a request for write-off following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 for the following applicable conditions:
Absence of records or documents to validate/support the claim and/or unreconciled reciprocal accounts
Death of the accountable officer/employee/debtor
Unknown whereabouts of the accountable officer/employee/debtor, and that he/she could not be located despite diligent efforts to find him/her
Incapacity to pay or insolvency
Exhaustion of all possible remedies by the Management to collect the receivables and to demand liquidation of cash advances and fund transfers
No pending case in court involving the subject dormant accounts.
In addition, we recommended that management take necessary action in case accountable officers failed to respond to the demand letters, particularly those who were already separated from the LGU.

We recommended the following to management:
Send confirmation letter to the Bureau of Treasury to determine the existence and validity of the Due from NGA account;
Send demand letters to the beneficiaries of the loan granted for livelihood program; and
Determine the status of collectability of these accounts and consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended the following to management:
The Property Officer to prepare the IIRUP for all PPEs found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer during the conduct of the physical inventory count; and
Prepare and implement the necessary procedures for the subsequent disposal of these unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed properties in accordance with Paragraph 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that management require the BAC to submit a written explanation narrating the actual situation on the above-mentioned procurements in order to establish the grounds that will warrant the use of negotiated procurement under emergency cases.

We recommended the following to management:
To prepare quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation using the prescribed format (Annex C) until such time that the Unified Reporting System for LGUs has been developed and can be used by the LGUs; and
Post the same in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the municipality within twenty (20) calendar days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended the following to management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the required reports and transaction documents within the reglementary period to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. No.1445 shall be imposed; and
Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents within the reglementary period.

We recommended that management issue designation order signed by the head of the agency to all collecting officers before they are issued accountable forms and perform collecting functions for the latter to be clothed with authority for acting as such.
We recommended that all AOs render the report of accountability of accountable forms at the end of the month for consolidation by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer:
Maintain the required RAAOs to facilitate monitoring of expenditures, verification of the balances of appropriations, and prevention of the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments and to serve as basis in preparing the SAAOB; and
Accomplish the Obligation Request properly, which shall be the basis of recording obligations in the registries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive be guided by the guidelines and procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the municipality;

The inventory committee conduct the physical inventory count in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and prepare the Report of Physical Count of PPE (RCPPE) duly approved by the Head of the Agency and distribute the same as follows:
Original	- COA Auditor, through the Accounting Division/Unit
Copy 2	- Accounting Division/Unit
Copy 3 	 - Supply and/or Property Division/Unit
Copy 4 - Inventory Officer/Committee

The GSO and Accounting Units undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records;

The GSO prepares the List of PPE found at station and Non-existing/Missing PPE using the format provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 based on the RPCPPE and follow the procedures for the disposition of non-existing / missing PPEs provided under Paragraph 7 of the Circular;

The Accounting Unit take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize PPE found at station and to recognize loss of PPE and to set-up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officer/personnel; and

Ensure that items of PPE reflected in the agency’s FS actually exist and if no complete information is available for the dropping of non-existent/missing properties, full disclosure must be made in the Notes to FS.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Accountant appropriately record the income generated through renting out LGU properties as Rent Income;

The Municipal Engineer submit all equipment lease contracts including approved Rental Request documents; and

The MEEDO submit schedule or summary pertaining to income generated from renting out properties owned by the LGU.

We recommended that management submit to the audit team a copy of the ordinance creating the SAGF, if any, and submit an explanation in writing for non-compliance with item 3.7 of the DBM LBC No. 125.

We recommended the following to management:

Direct all concerned personnel to submit copies of POs to the GSO immediately upon perfection for monitoring purposes;

The GSO retains a copy of the signed POs and submit to the COA Auditor within 5 working days, irrespective of amount, together with the related supporting documents; and

The GSO furnishes the COA Auditor with copies of delivery documents within 24 hours acceptance of goods and services.

We recommended the following to management:

Promulgate rules, regulations and guidelines on rental of heavy equipment and other LGU properties for proper recording and reporting, and to safeguard said properties from possible loss or damage; and

Submit documents, if any, to support the recorded rental income for CY 2020 and all other transactions of the LGU on leasing out LGU properties.

We recommended the following to the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat:

Indicate in the RFQ all the necessary contents and information relating to the goods or supplies to be procured as prescribed in RA 9184 and Procurement Manual for LGUs.

Submit a written explanation why the quoted amount of the winning bidder is always exactly the same with the ABC as indicated in the PR despite the lack of this information in the RFQ; and

Always double check computation to avoid errors affecting correctness of other related documents.

We recommended that the GFPS recommends to the SB the passing of the ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, we recommend that the ordinance include provisions on the following: measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; imposition of fines; and creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We reiterate our recommendation that Management require the Project Accountant and concerned personnel to:

Resolve the recurring delays noted and strictly observe the reglementary period in the submission of subsequent accounting and project reports in compliance with existing rules and regulations; and

Immediately submit all unsubmitted accounting and project reports.

We recommend that management create/strengthen GFPS or similar GAD mechanism to catalyze and accelerate gender mainstreaming within the agency to promote women’s human rights and eliminate gender discrimination in their system, structure, policies and programs.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee of the municipality observe an equal footing in applying the non-discretionary pass/fail criterion during the opening of bids and carefully evaluate the bids of the prospective bidder and religiously consider the completeness of the bid as well as computational errors and omissions to ensure fair and competitive bid. Prospective bidders who failed to comply with the eligibility, technical and financial requirements should be declared “failed” or “disqualified” to ensure effective, efficient and competitive bidding process.
We recommend that granting of cash advances shall be in accordance with the guidelines.

It is recommended that the officials responsible for the preparation of POs indicate all the necessary information pertaining to purchase and submit the same within the prescribed period; and to notify the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours after deliveries of procured items to enable the Auditor to conduct timely inspections of the same.
observations.reason: The responsible officials still overlook to indicate the basic information in some of the POs as revealed in the post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities in the disbursements of public funds, observed proper internal control and stop the practice of issuing checks if the DVs lack signatures and provide proper numbering of all disbursement vouchers in accordance with the provisions.
observations.reason: It is partially implemented because the management complied in ensuring that all DVs are signed before issuing checks but failed to number some DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer pay electric bill on time to avoid incurrence of penalty/surcharges. For the surcharges which have been due already, the persons responsible for its incurrence shall be made to refund for the same.

We recommend that the Auditor be furnished with all documentary requirements of the abovementioned transactions. Require the Human Resources Officer and the Municipal Engineer to submit the Program of Works and the payrolls for labor payment with the corresponding job orders and the Project Cost Sheet and Accomplishment Report for each and every voucher claim.
observations.reason: There is unfaithfulne ss and inconsistency in the long run implementati on of the recommendati ons.

observations.recommendation: We recommend, to avoid possible sanctions for violation of Section 89 of PD 1445, strict adherence to the limitations of cash advances as cited above. Also, the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances should effect immediate settlement of the same and additional cash advance/s should not be allowed to any official or employee unless previous cash advance/s given to him/her is settled.
observations.reason: There are officials who still opt to leave their obligations outstanding despite the demand from the accountant and a weak internal control in granting Cash Advances to officials with unliquidated CAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Representation and Transportation Allowances (RATA) shall only be paid if no fund and cash overdraft is existing and will be incurred, and appropriate charges be filed if warranted, to concerned Officials for certifying and approving such irregular expenditures.

We recommend, that for financial statements be fairly presented, all expenses should be recognized when incurred; that expenses be appropriately classified; and as such, financial charges for interest expenses should be properly recognized in the books of accounts.

We recommend that to promote good governance through transparency and accountability, the LDRRMFIP and the Report of Utilization of LDRRMF shall be prepared and submitted to the LGU Auditor for the timely conduct of its mandated audit.
Refund of ₱5,018.52 must be required from the concerned officials who had caused the delay of the payments/ remittance to Pag-IBIG Fund (HMDF).

Refund must be required from the signatory of the checks in the amount of ₱8,800.00 in compliance with Sec. 1 of Batas pambansa Blg. 22. The Municipal Treasurer must also be directed to refrain from issuing checks when no sufficient funds are available. Proper control on cash in bank and book balances must be ensured to prevent unnecessary wastage or loss of government funds.

Stop the payment of claims for solicitation for the benefit of any religious sect or activity nor any undertaking or purpose private in character as stipulated in Section 335 of RA 7160 And Art. 454(a) of its IRR.

Require the Municipal Accountant to verify as to the actual number of days rendered basing on the duly approved daily time record for the computation of the amount to be collected on the rates of subsistence allowance that is ₱50.00 per day but not to exceed ₱1,500.00 per month equivalent to 30 days service.

The municipal government must comply with the guidelines in the granting, utilization, accounting and auditing of the funds released to Non-Governmental Organizations/Peoples Organizations (NGOs/POs) embodied under COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

The officials concerned must be careful in the verification for approval any claims to be in conformity with the provisions of law, rules and regulations with all pertinent supporting documents to prove its validity, propriety and legality.

The Municipal officials who take charge of the conduct of trainings and seminar should comply with the provisions mandated under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007 and assure completeness of supporting documents as attached to the claims.

Stop the payment of honoraria for BNS charged to the municipality and comply with the policies provided under LBC No. 63 dated October 22, 1996 with the subject 'Position Classification and Compensation of Barangay Officials and Personnel'.

Require the concerned officials to pay honoraria in conformity with Section 288 and 289 of GAAM Vol. I. Budget Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007 and 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007 provides Guidelines on the Grant of Honoraria to Lecturers, Resource Persons, Coordinators and Facilitators and Guidelines on the Grant of Honoraria Due in Government Special Projects respectively.
Require the certification of the District Supervisor on all vouchers or payrolls as to validity, propriety and legality of the claims involved for Special Education Fund.

The Head of the Agency should set limit as to the allowable amount for each official duly permitted to acquire a unit. Any excess of the limitation set must be settled personally by the officials concerned.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer that upon approval of any payroll/voucher for payment of wages to be paid in cash shall be drawn with a cash advance by the designated disbursing officer. No check shall be issued to claimant unless all of the claimants in the payroll need to be paid in check. The Municipal Accountant must see to it that the required documents on the claims for wages are complete before certifying the voucher/payroll as to the completeness of documents. The required report as required of the payment must be complied.
Require the Local School Board to prepare a budget duly approved by.

Require the Municipal Treasurer to apply strategies to properly control, update and monitor the issuances of accountable forms and verification of its collection. The liquidating officer must be instructed to comply strictly the verification process on collection and accountable forms as provided under Sec. 30 of NGAS for LGU Vol. 1.
observations.managementAction: Memo was issued but was not strictly implemented
observations.reason: The issuing officer is not strictly implementin g what has been recommended.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant, to submit the trial balances with its financial statements according to the provisions of Section 76 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I, to facilitate the calendar year immediate submission of the required reports considering that the deadline set had already lapsed. Also, explanation must be submitted by the concerned official why no administrative and/or criminal sanction could be taken against him for failure to comply with the requirements of law.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travel were already prepared but not submitted to COA office.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly the provision provided under Section 7.3 of COA Circular No. 94-006 dated February 17, 1994. He must submit all transaction documents within the reglementary period so not to obstruct the early action of the Auditor on those transactions.
observations.managementAction: Memo was issued for compliance by Municipal Administrator
observations.reason: Some transaction documents are not forwarded immediately by the Treasurer to the Accountant upon payment.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to enforce deduction of ten (10%) percent retention as warranty security for any purchase of supplies or equipment or to post a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least ten (10%) percent of the contract price. The warranty provisions of R.A. 9184 do not provide exemption for a purchase of reconditioned equipment.

Require the Head of Offices concerned to comply with the requirements as mandated under COA Circular No. 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006 on the execution of their functions on accounting policies to facilitate the systematic flow of transactions and completeness of documents as required per transaction.
All Purchase Orders as a contract entered into by the municipal government of Dimataling with supplier/dealer for the delivery of good/supplies must be approved only by the Municipal Mayor.
Require the agency to submit the GAD plan and budget to DILG to comply with the provisions of law as provided for under the IRR of 9710.

Local Officials concerned must have to transfer the remaining amount to the Special Trust Fund Account opened solely for the unexpended LDRRMF which had already accumulated for the last three years beginning 2010 to 2012. A latest issuance per Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 by NDRRMC, DBM and DILG has been issued for LGUs on the Allocation and Utilization of the LDRRMF.

A new team to constitute the inventory committee should be created to conduct the annual physical inventory of all assets under PPE to ensure fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements. In addition, the Municipal Treasurer must be required to prepare her property cards and the Municipal Accountant the corresponding equipment and property ledger cards in order to reconcile the records with both offices. Further, reclassification of obsolete and unserviceable assets no longer used to “Other Assets” account should be made.
observations.managementAction: The annual physical inventory has been conducted already. An inventory committee has been reconstitute d last July 2013, with the following composition; Chairman: Artemio Carbero Members: Archie Dalagon, Janice Lomocso. The Municipal Treasurer is now starting to prepare the property Cards while the Municipal Accountant prepares the corresponding equipment and property ledger cards in order to reconcile the records with both offices. Obsolete and unserviceable assets have been classified to other assets.
observations.reason: The inventory team has not completed its physical count of all property, plant and equipment of the LGU and no report has been submitted yet.

observations.recommendation: Payment of procured items of any kind shall be made directly to the supplier/dealer. This is to ensure that, the proper procedures on the procurement system as provided in RA 9184 will be strictly followed. All the necessary documents shall first be complied and completed to ensure the approval of disbursement vouchers before payments are made. Require the claimants who paid the expenses, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to submit the Program of Works with the Purchase Request together with the details of items to be procured for all repairs and maintenance, the pre-inspection report, all documents pertaining to the procurement process to include BAC resolutions with the Notice of Award, PO, post-inspection report, acceptance report, waste material report, RIS, and the payrolls for labor payment with the corresponding job orders and the Project Cost Sheet for each and every voucher claim so with all other procurements made. Explanation is also required why payment from collection was allowed and transactions were perfected without the required supporting documents.
observations.reason: The practice on paying through reimbursem ent has been minimized. The payment of procured items was already in the name of the supplier/dea ler.

observations.recommendation: The concerned officials must exert extra effort to prepare the required procurement plan using the PPMP per vehicles and equipment identifying the details of the parts needed in compliance with the requirement of the budget process per Step 2.3 of UBOM and 7.2 of IRR-A of RA 9184. Also all Purchase Request relating to the procurement of spare parts must give identity to a specific vehicle or equipment to which it will be utilized and correspondingly the waste material reports must likewise mention the same.
observations.reason: The PPMP for each vehicle were already prepared but the APP summarizing the same were not furnished to COA.

observations.recommendation: Require the Drivers and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control and evaluation.
observations.reason: The Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travel were already prepared but not submitted to COA office.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to utilize only regular and bonded Revenue Collectors to undertake the collection. She must design control system for the liquidation process and issuance of accountable forms. The Liquidating Officer must see to it that in every liquidation of all revenue collectors, all issued OR’s are monitored and reflected in the liquidation report, as well as all balances on hand per previous verified reports are properly carried as beginning balance for the current report.
observations.reason: Casual Employees were still utilized because of lack of personnel and all were bonded as allowed by the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant and concerned officials to fill-up the relevant information contain in the said forms for each has its purpose. The document must be signed as approved only when the required data are complete.
observations.reason: There were still accounting forms not duly filled-up as required.
